pathways.title: Adult Liver Disease (LFT)
pathways.description: Raised ALT (viral/autoimmune/metabolic screen + FIB-4/Fibroscan/ELF cascade), raised ALP ± GGT, alcohol-related and NAFLD liver risk assessment, synthetic-failure/malignancy 2WW, isolated bilirubin (Gilbert's), ferritin/iron overload. Age-modified FIB-4 scoring.
pathways.source: BSG 2017 abnormal liver blood tests; NICE NG49/NG50; MIB2016
pathways.note: Nottingham & Nottinghamshire variant. Different from the NW London Abnormal LFT pathway (uses FIB-4 cascade and NUH local thresholds) — both surface on raised LFTs; clinician picks the local pathway.

pathways.title: Abnormal LFT
pathways.description: Hepatic and cholestatic patterns, Gilbert's syndrome, NAFLD, alcohol-related abnormality, isolated bilirubin or ALP elevation.

pathways.title: Abnormal FBC
pathways.description: Anaemia (with MCV stratification, ferritin/B12/folate branching), polycythaemia, macrocytosis, neutropenia, neutrophilia, lymphopenia, lymphocytosis, eosinophilia, monocytosis, thrombocytosis, thrombocytopenia.
pathways.authors: Dr Sarah Morgan, Dr Sajir Mohamedbhai

pathways.title: NWL Anaemia
pathways.description: Adult anaemia (Hb <130 M / <114 F) with strict iron deficiency criteria (Hb + MCV <83.5 + ferritin <10 F / <20 M) and sex/menopausal-status GI investigation routing. Flow diagram highlights route taken.
pathways.note: Different thresholds from NCL FBC pathway — clinician picks which applies.

pathways.title: Adult Iron Deficiency
pathways.description: Adult iron deficiency primary care pathway. CV stability triage, 2WW gastroenterology criteria, routine gastro referral, non-GI bleeding routes, iron replacement and follow-up. SVG flow diagram with per-rule active path highlighting (post-v5 fixes).
pathways.note: Patched post-build to fix empty-input handling, missing SVG arrows, hardcoded active path, and sex-conditional blocking. Triggered the v5 master prompt enforceability tightenings.

router_principles: Router matches trigger_variables to entered fields by name only.
Router does NOT make clinical decisions — actual logic runs inside each pathway tool.
Where multiple pathways trigger on the same result (e.g. NCL FBC + NWL Anaemia on Hb), both surface and the clinician picks.
